narrative.springFall: May and September are Kraków at its absolute finest. Spring brings the Planty park ring into leaf, the Wawel hill greening above the Vistula, and the student energy of Juwenalia spilling through the streets, while Museum Night opens the city's collections after dark. Fall delivers golden light across the Rynek Główny, mild afternoons for wandering Kazimierz, and the Conrad literary and Unsound experimental-music festivals. Temperatures sit around 14–20°C—ideal for cobblestoned lanes and café terraces. Aim for May or September, when crowds thin, prices ease, and the largest medieval market square in Europe feels like your own.
narrative.summer: July and August bring long days, packed café terraces, and a calendar overflowing with festivals—the Jewish Culture Festival in Kazimierz, the Summer Jazz Festival, and the mid-August Pierogi Festival on the Rynek. The trade-off is peak tourist season: St. Mary's Basilica and the Cloth Hall draw dense queues, the Main Market Square hums until late, and hotel prices climb. Temperatures reach 23–25°C with occasional thunderstorms. If you visit, start early at Wawel, retreat to the Vistula boulevards or Zakrzówek in the afternoon heat, and embrace the late-night buzz of Plac Nowy when the day-trippers leave.
narrative.winter: November through February reveals Kraków's magical side. Snow settles on the Gothic spires, the Christmas Market on Rynek Główny fills the square with mulled wine, obwarzanki, and oscypek, and the UNESCO-listed Kraków szopki (nativity-crib) contest crowns the first Thursday of December. You can finally hear the hejnał trumpet call without fighting crowds. Temperatures hover -4 to 3°C—cold but manageable with layers. Hotel prices drop sharply, and the city feels more local. This is the season for candlelit cellars, cozy milk bars, and warm pierogi, with Wawel Castle silhouetted against winter skies.

seasonalNeighborhoods.reason: the blooming park ring, Wawel views, and reopening café terraces before the rush
festival energy, zapiekanka stalls, and late-night courtyard bars
